speaker: LUCIANA
text: And may it be that you have quite forgot

speaker: LUCIANA
text: A husband’s office? Shall, Antipholus,

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Even in the spring of love thy love-springs rot?

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Shall love, in building, grow so ruinous?

speaker: LUCIANA
text: If you did wed my sister for her wealth,

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Then for her wealth’s sake use her with more kindness.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Or if you like elsewhere, do it by stealth —

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Muffle your false love with some show of blindness.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Let not my sister read it in your eye;

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Be not thy tongue thy own shame’s orator;

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Look sweet, speak fair, become disloyalty;

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Apparel vice like virtue’s harbinger.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Bear a fair presence, though your heart be tainted.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Teach sin the carriage of a holy saint.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Be secret-false. What need she be acquainted?

speaker: LUCIANA
text: What simple thief brags of his own attaint?

speaker: LUCIANA
text: ’Tis double wrong to truant with your bed

speaker: LUCIANA
text: And let her read it in thy looks at board.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Shame hath a bastard fame, well managèd;

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Ill deeds is doubled with an evil word.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Alas, poor women, make us but believe,

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Being compact of credit, that you love us.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Though others have the arm, show us the sleeve;

speaker: LUCIANA
text: We in your motion turn, and you may move us.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Then, gentle brother, get you in again.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Comfort my sister, cheer her, call her wife.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: ’Tis holy sport to be a little vain

speaker: LUCIANA
text: When the sweet breath of flattery conquers strife.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Sweet mistress—what your name is else I know not,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nor by what wonder you do hit of mine—

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Less in your knowledge and your grace you show not

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Than our Earth’s wonder, more than Earth divine.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Teach me, dear creature, how to think and speak.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Lay open to my earthy gross conceit,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Smothered in errors, feeble, shallow, weak,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: The folded meaning of your words’ deceit.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Against my soul’s pure truth why labor you

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: To make it wander in an unknown field?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Are you a god? Would you create me new?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Transform me, then, and to your power I’ll yield.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: But if that I am I, then well I know

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Your weeping sister is no wife of mine,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nor to her bed no homage do I owe.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Far more, far more, to you do I decline.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: O, train me not, sweet mermaid, with thy note

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: To drown me in thy sister’s flood of tears.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Sing, Siren, for thyself, and I will dote.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Spread o’er the silver waves thy golden hairs,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And as a bed I’ll take them and there lie,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And in that glorious supposition think

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: He gains by death that hath such means to die.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Let love, being light, be drownèd if she sink.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: What, are you mad that you do reason so?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Not mad, but mated—how, I do not know.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: It is a fault that springeth from your eye.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: For gazing on your beams, fair sun, being by.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Gaze when you should, and that will clear your sight.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: As good to wink, sweet love, as look on night.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Why call you me “love”? Call my sister so.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thy sister’s sister.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: That’s my sister.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: No,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: It is thyself, mine own self’s better part,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Mine eye’s clear eye, my dear heart’s dearer heart,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: My food, my fortune, and my sweet hope’s aim,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: My sole Earth’s heaven, and my heaven’s claim.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: All this my sister is, or else should be.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Call thyself “sister,” sweet, for I am thee.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thee will I love, and with thee lead my life;

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thou hast no husband yet, nor I no wife.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Give me thy hand.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: O soft, sir. Hold you still.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: I’ll fetch my sister to get her goodwill.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Why, how now, Dromio.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where runn’st thou so fast?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Do you know me, sir? Am I

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Dromio? Am I your man? Am I myself?

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Thou art Dromio, thou art

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: my man, thou art thyself.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I am an ass, I am a woman’s

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: man, and besides myself.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What woman’s man? And

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: how besides thyself?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Marry, sir, besides myself I am

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: due to a woman, one that claims me, one that

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: haunts me, one that will have me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What claim lays she to thee?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Marry, sir, such claim as you

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: would lay to your horse, and she would have me as

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: a beast; not that I being a beast she would have me,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: but that she, being a very beastly creature, lays

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: claim to me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What is she?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: A very reverend body, ay, such a

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: one as a man may not speak of without he say

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: “sir-reverence.” I have but lean luck in the match,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: and yet is she a wondrous fat marriage.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: How dost thou mean a “fat

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: marriage”?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Marry, sir, she’s the kitchen

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: wench, and all grease, and I know not what use to

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: put her to but to make a lamp of her and run from

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: her by her own light. I warrant her rags and the

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: tallow in them will burn a Poland winter. If she lives

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: till doomsday, she’ll burn a week longer than the

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: whole world.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What complexion is she of?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Swart like my shoe, but her face

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: nothing like so clean kept. For why? She sweats. A

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: man may go overshoes in the grime of it.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: That’s a fault that water will

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: mend.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: No, sir, ’tis in grain; Noah’s flood

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: could not do it.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What’s her name?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Nell, sir, but her name and

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: three quarters—that’s an ell and three quarters—

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: will not measure her from hip to hip.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Then she bears some

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: breadth?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: No longer from head to foot than

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: from hip to hip. She is spherical, like a globe. I

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: could find out countries in her.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: In what part of her body

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: stands Ireland?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Marry, sir, in her buttocks. I

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: found it out by the bogs.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where Scotland?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I found it by the barrenness,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: hard in the palm of the hand.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where France?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: In her forehead, armed and

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: reverted, making war against her heir.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where England?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I looked for the chalky cliffs, but

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I could find no whiteness in them. But I guess it

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: stood in her chin, by the salt rheum that ran

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: between France and it.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where Spain?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Faith, I saw it not, but I felt it hot

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: in her breath.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where America, the Indies?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: O, sir, upon her nose, all o’erembellished

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: with rubies, carbuncles, sapphires,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: declining their rich aspect to the hot breath of

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Spain, who sent whole armadas of carracks to be

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: ballast at her nose.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where stood Belgia, the

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Netherlands?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: O, sir, I did not look so low. To

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: conclude: this drudge or diviner laid claim to me,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: called me Dromio, swore I was assured to her, told

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: me what privy marks I had about me, as the mark

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: of my shoulder, the mole in my neck, the great wart

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: on my left arm, that I, amazed, ran from her as a

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: witch.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And, I think, if my breast had not been made of faith, and my heart of steel,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: She had transformed me to a curtal dog and made me turn i’ th’ wheel.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Go, hie thee presently. Post to the road.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: An if the wind blow any way from shore,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I will not harbor in this town tonight.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If any bark put forth, come to the mart,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Where I will walk till thou return to me.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If everyone knows us, and we know none,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: ’Tis time, I think, to trudge, pack, and be gone.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: As from a bear a man would run for life,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: So fly I from her that would be my wife.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: There’s none but witches do inhabit here,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: And therefore ’tis high time that I were hence.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: She that doth call me husband, even my soul

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Doth for a wife abhor. But her fair sister,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Possessed with such a gentle sovereign grace,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Of such enchanting presence and discourse,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Hath almost made me traitor to myself.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: But lest myself be guilty to self wrong,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I’ll stop mine ears against the mermaid’s song.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Master Antipholus.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Ay, that’s my name.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I know it well, sir. Lo, here’s the chain.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I thought to have ta’en you at the Porpentine;

speaker: ANGELO
text: The chain unfinished made me stay thus long.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What is your will that I shall do with this?

speaker: ANGELO
text: What please yourself, sir. I have made it for you.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: Made it for me, sir? I bespoke it not.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Not once, nor twice, but twenty times you have.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Go home with it, and please your wife withal,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And soon at supper time I’ll visit you

speaker: ANGELO
text: And then receive my money for the chain.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I pray you, sir, receive the money now,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: For fear you ne’er see chain nor money more.

speaker: ANGELO
text: You are a merry man, sir. Fare you well.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: What I should think of this I cannot tell,

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: But this I think: there’s no man is so vain

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: That would refuse so fair an offered chain.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I see a man here needs not live by shifts

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: When in the streets he meets such golden gifts.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: I’ll to the mart, and there for Dromio stay.

speaker:
ANTIPHOLUS
               
              OF
               
              SYRACUSE
text: If any ship put out, then straight away.
